'''Adam Walne''' (born 3 October 1990), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Waldo"''', is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Barrow Raiders]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|RFL championship 1]].

He played for [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] in the [[Super League]], spending time on loan from the [[Salford Red Devils|Reds]] at [[Workington Town]] and the [[Barrow Raiders]]. In 2018 he spent time on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at the [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Walne was born in [[Greater Manchester, England]]. He is the brother of [[Jordan Walne]] who is also a rugby league footballer.

==Playing career==
===Salford===
Walne joined Salford from amateur club Leyland ARLFC, from [[Leyland, Lancashire|Leyland]] near [[Preston, Lancashire|Preston]]. He made his début for the club on 20 August 2012 in a [[Super League]] match against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at the [[DW Stadium]].

====Dual registration====
In 2012 and 2013, Walne was dual registered with [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] club [[Workington Town]]. He also spent some time dual registered with [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] of [[League 1 (rugby league)|Championship 1]] in 2014.

===Huddersfield===
In September 2017, it was announced that Walne had joined the [[Huddersfield Giants]] on a three-year deal from 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41427109|title=Adam Walne: Huddersfield Giants sign prop from Salford Red Devils|publisher=BBC Sport|date=28 September 2017|accessdate=7 January 2018}}</ref>

===Barrow Raiders===
It was announced in September 2020 that Walne would join his brother at [[Barrow Raiders]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nwemail.co.uk/sport/18693943.brothers-reunion-good-turn-says-adam-walne-super-league-prop-signs-two-year-barrow-raiders-deal/|title=Brothers reunion too good to turn down says Adam Walne as Super League prop signs two year Barrow Raiders deal|publisher=The Mail|accessdate=15 Dec 2020}}</ref> They both left Barrow Raiders in February 2022.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.barrowrlfc.com/latest-news/adam-and-jordan-walne-depart-from-the-raiders/ |title=Adam and Jordan Walne depart Barrow Raiders |website=Barrow Raiders |date=28 February 2022 |access-date=6 June 2024}}</ref>
